topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: At 1.16 with only 4 RBs rostered, Price is the highest-upside RB likely available — RSP DoT 83.5 (RB6 on the board) with ceiling 'as high as RBs 2-5' and ADP 16.05 lines up almost exactly with this slot. Cheapest path to a starting-caliber dynasty back without trading up.

topTargets.name: Denzel Boston
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 83.1 with 'quality pick in late first' note and ADP 16.11 — a perfect 1.16 value where RSP and consensus agree. Boundary size profile diversifies a WR room that already runs 7 deep but lacks a clear young alpha.

topTargets.name: Kenyon Sadiq
topTargets.reasoning: ADP 13.11 says he may not even reach 1.16, but if the run on RBs/WRs at the turn pushes him down, the Brock Bowers high-end comp and combine athleticism are too good to pass — even with 5 TEs, the room lacks an ascending young athlete.

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 83.9 with explicit 'worth second-round consideration' note; ADP 17.20. If Price and Boston both go in the 1.13-1.15 range, Cooper is the next tier of upside WR with a Deebo Samuel ceiling.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Primary 3.11 target — ADP 25.83 lands right in this range. RSP DoT 84.1 with 'second- or third-round pick in rookie drafts' note makes him a textbook value for an RB-light roster grabbing depth.

capPosture: Comfortable at $33.9M used with $11.1M space — can absorb a 1.16 active-roster rookie contract and still flex 3.11 to taxi if needed.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.4 with 'on cusp of Starter tier' and classic move-TE profile; ADP 21.25 makes him a 3.11 reach but the medium RSP-affinity GM might trust Waldman's grade over consensus. Adds an athletic young TE to a stale 5-deep room.

summary: Computer Jocks pick at the back of round 1 and middle of round 3 with healthy cap and a roster heavy on WR/TE depth but thin at RB. The brief blends RSP and ADP boards (medium affinity) to target an RB at 1.16 — Jadarian Price is the bullseye where both boards converge — with Denzel Boston as the WR fallback if RBs run early. At 3.11, expect another RB swing (Singleton) or a Cooper Jr./Stowers upside dart, with one of the three 3.11 candidates likely going to taxi to preserve cap flexibility.
